assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md: 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]' — only 2.1.x versions. RHTPA 2.2.0 removed, RHTPA 2.1.1 added.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md: Both tasks target 2.1.x stream — upstream on release/0.3.z, downstream on rhtpa-release.0.3.z. Explicit section 'Why No 2.2.x Remediation' explains all 2.2.x versions ship h2 >= 0.4.8.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md: 'no stream suffix — unscoped' and 'it covers all configured version streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x)'. version-impact.md lists all 7 versions across both streams.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md: 'A cross-stream impact notice is **not needed** for this issue. TC-8004 is unscoped (no stream suffix), so it covers all streams by definition. There are no other streams outside the issue scope to notify about.'
